definition: The principle that phenomena arise dependent upon conditions.
notes: Central doctrinal framework explaining how dissatisfaction arises and how it can be quenched through the ending of conditions.

context_rules.context: In most project translations and doctrinal discussion
When referring to the standard English technical term for external comparison
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when noting conventional terminology.

example_phrases.translation: with ignorance as condition, choices arise
when this is, this comes to be
example_phrases.notes: Core formula associated with dependent arising.
